```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import { getMarketplace, clearMarketplace } from '../services';
import { MarketplaceStatus } from '../models';

export function useMarketplace() {
  const marketplace = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.marketplace));

  const requestGetMarketplace = useCallback(
    async (params: Record<string, string>) => {
      await getMarketplace(params);
    },
    []
  );

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearMarketplace();
  }, []);

  return {
    data: marketplace.data,
    isLoading: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.LOADING,
    isError: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.ERROR,
    requestGetMarketplace,
    clear,
  };
}
```

- 12.0 Comprehensive Migration Verification Audit (2026-01-27)

AUDIT OBJECTIVE: Verify that logic was maintained during Redux→Zustand migration.
The migration should ONLY change infrastructure (state management), NOT business logic or behavior.

AUDIT METHOD: 10 specialized agents performed line-by-line analysis of all hooks, services, stores, and screens.

12.1 CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND (PRE-EXISTING - NOT CAUSED BY MIGRATION)
================================================================================

These issues existed in the original card-place codebase. They are NOT migration errors but should be documented for future reference. Per migration rules, these should NOT be fixed during migration (no logic changes allowed).

### 12.1.1 rescue.service.ts - Response Validation Logic (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/services/rescue.service.ts:23
Issue: `if (response?.data?.status === 'OK' || response?.status?.message)` uses OR instead of AND
Impact: Any response with a message object could be treated as success
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.2 InvoiceCashbackSummary - campaingId Typo (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx:97
Issue: Route param named `campaingId` (misspelled) but payload field is `campaignId` (correct)
Impact: Field name mismatch could cause data loss if caller uses correct spelling
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.3 InvoiceCashbackOnboarding - pointsQuantity Always 0 (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx:51, parameter.store.ts:26
Issue: pointsQuantity initialized to 0 and never updated (setPointsQuantity never called)
Impact: Display shows "Cada 0 pontos valem R$ 0.00" always
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.4 InvoiceCashbackPoints - Dual conversionFactor Sources (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx:54
Issue: Uses route.params.conversionFactor (defaults to 0) instead of usePoints() hook value
Impact: If route param missing, all conversions silently fail (multiply by 0)
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.5 ExtractRewardProgram - Pagination Race Condition (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/index.ts:285-287
Issue: handleScroll uses refs instead of reactive state, allowing multiple concurrent requests
Impact: Duplicate API requests, data merging bugs, page counter desync
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.6 ExtractRewardProgram - Data Grouping Lost on Append (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:97
Issue: appendExtractData overwrites groupedTransactions with only new page's groups
Impact: Date grouping destroyed on pagination, UI shows incomplete grouped data
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.7 ExtractRewardProgram - "A expirar" Tab Empty (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/view.tsx:176-180
Issue: Second tab renders empty fragment <></>
Impact: Tab is non-functional
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.8 parameter.store.ts - Missing Error Field (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/parameter.store.ts:22-28, 8-15
Issue: No error field in state, no setError method in actions
Impact: Parameter fetch errors cannot be stored or displayed
Status: PRE-EXISTING - design inconsistency from original architecture

### 12.1.9 extract.store.ts - setBalanceStatus Doesn't Clear Error (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:120-123
Issue: setBalanceStatus doesn't clear error on LOADING (unlike setExtractStatus)
Impact: Stale error messages remain visible on retry
Status: PRE-EXISTING - inconsistency within same store

### 12.1.10 products.service.ts - shouldFetchProducts Missing SUCCESS Guard (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/services/products.service.ts:7-10
Issue: Only checks LOADING status, not SUCCESS - allows refetch of cached data
Impact: Unnecessary refetching, no cache respect
Status: PRE-EXISTING - design decision, not a bug

Animated.Value.setValue()/stopAnimation()/loop().start() operate imperatively outside React reconciliation. 3 review agents: 2 approved, 1 flagged missing useEffect cleanup as resource leak on unmount — PRE-EXISTING code quality issue, not an infinite loop, documented below as future task.)

- [x] Deeply and recursively review the possible react hooks inside "src/atomic/templates/BasicScreen/index.tsx" against possible infinite loops. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. 5 hooks analyzed: useNavigation (stable context ref), useNavigationState (returns primitive string — stable by value), useCallback(nativeGoBackHandler, [hasNativeGoBack, nativeGoBackFn]) (both deps are primitives or external; if parent doesn't memoize nativeGoBackFn, causes BackHandler effect churn but NOT a loop since addEventListener/remove are native bridge calls with no React state mutation), useEffect([props.hasError]) fires eventModalView which is analytics-only with no state side effects (eslint-disable on currentRouteName is intentional — including it would re-fire analytics on navigation while error persists), useEffect([nativeGoBackHandler]) manages BackHandler listener imperatively with cleanup — no state feedback path. handleGoToHome is not memoized but only passed to pure FC BasicScreenView (no loop since child re-render doesn't feed back). 3 review agents: all APPROVED. Observations (non-blocking): unstable nativeGoBackFn from parent causes unnecessary native re-subscriptions, handleGoToHome could be useCallback-wrapped if View were ever React.memo'd.)

- [x] Deeply and recursively review the possible react hooks inside "src/hooks/tracking/index.tsx" against possible infinite loops. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. Single useEffect with [isFocused] dependency — a primitive boolean from React Navigation's focus/blur events. Effect only fires on actual focus transitions (true<->false). dispatchEventFocus is an inline closure capturing route, analytic, title, handleAfterDispatch — intentionally excluded from deps via eslint-disable (adding it would fire analytics on every render). Stale closure is benign: all 5 callers pass static string literals, and values are fresh at focus transitions since React re-renders before running effects. handleAfterDispatch is not passed by any current caller; even if it called setState in parent, isFocused would remain unchanged so the effect would not re-fire. The if(isFocused) guard provides a second layer preventing execution on blur. 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict. No corrections needed.)

- [x] Deeply and recursively review the possible react hooks inside "src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx" against possible infinite loops. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. 7 hooks analyzed: usePoints() (custom hook with 3 useShallow selectors + 5 useCallback([]) singletons — all referentially stable), useState(0) refresh with no setter (constant 0, dead code), useRef(false) hasFocused (ref guard for analytics), useFocusEffect(useCallback(fn, [])) (stable callback, fires once per focus with ref guard, no state mutations), useNavigation() (stable context ref), useCallback(requestAll, [requestParameterization, requestPointsProducts]) (both deps are useCallback([]) singletons — frozen), useEffect([requestParameterization, refresh]) (fires exactly once on mount — requestParameterization is useCallback([]) singleton and refresh is constant 0; three-layer defense: stable deps + immutable refresh + service LOADING/SUCCESS guard). Store transitions via useShallow cause bounded re-renders (max 3: IDLE→LOADING→SUCCESS/ERROR) that never re-trigger the effect. Non-issues noted: dead refresh state (vestigial), invoiceList() called twice per render (minor perf), non-memoized handlers (child not React.memo), no useEffect cleanup for async (safe with Zustand external state). 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict.)

- [x] Deeply and recursively review the possible react hooks inside "src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx" against possible infinite loops. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. 14 hooks analyzed (including nested hooks from usePoints and useScreenViewTracking). Key findings: (1) useEffect([requestParameterization]) fires exactly once on mount — requestParameterization is useCallback([]) singleton, referentially stable; getParameterPointsProgram service has LOADING/SUCCESS guard preventing re-entry; store transitions (IDLE→LOADING→SUCCESS) cause bounded 3 re-renders but never re-trigger the effect. (2) Second useFocusEffect with [setPointsValue, setReaisValue] deps is safe — React guarantees useState setters are referentially stable, making this equivalent to useCallback([]); state resets fire on focus/blur only (not on re-renders) and no effect depends on pointsValue/reaisValue/pointsError/reaisError. (3) First useFocusEffect with [] deps + hasFocused ref guard — fires analytics once per focus, no state mutations. (4) useScreenViewTracking internal useEffect([isFocused]) — fires on boolean primitive toggle, analytics only, no state mutations, eslint-disable on dispatchEventFocus is intentional. (5) useMemo([conversionFactor]) — depends on route params (frozen per navigation entry). Non-blocking observations: invoiceList() creates new array every render (could be useMemo'd), Intl.NumberFormat instantiated every render, 8 unmemoized handler functions, Dimensions.get('window') called every render instead of useWindowDimensions(), duplicate analytics on focus (useScreenViewTracking V2 + useFocusEffect V3 — may be intentional dual-system). 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict with HIGH confidence.)

### 2.2 Export from hooks/index.ts ✅ DONE

This hook REPLACES all pointsReducer + PointsActions usage.

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import {
  getPointsProducts,
  clearPointsProducts,
  getPointsBalance,
  getParameterPointsProgram,
} from '../services';
import {
  PointsProductsStatus,
  PointsBalanceStatus,
  ParameterPointsProgramStatus,
} from '../models';

export function usePoints() {
  // Products from products store
  const pointsProducts = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsProducts));
  const { pointsProductsActions } = useRootStore.getState();

  // Balance from extract store (pointsBalance)
  const pointsBalance = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsBalance));

  // Parameterization from parameter store
  const parameterPointsProgram = useRootStore(
    useShallow((state) => state.parameterPointsProgram)
  );

  const requestInitialData = useCallback(async () => {
    await Promise.all([
      getPointsBalance(),
      getPointsProducts(),
      getParameterPointsProgram(),
    ]);
  }, []);

  const requestPointsBalance = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsBalance();
  }, []);

  const requestPointsProducts = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  const requestParameterization = useCallback(async () => {
    await getParameterPointsProgram();
  }, []);

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  return {
    // Balance (from extract store - pointsBalance)
    points: pointsBalance.points,
    isLoadingBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.ERROR,

    // Products (from products store)
    products: pointsProducts.products,
    isLoadingProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.ERROR,

    // Parameterization (from parameter store)
    pointsQuantity: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,
    conversionFactor: parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor,
    isLoadingParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,

    // Actions
    requestPointsBalance,
    requestPointsProducts,
    requestParameterization,
    requestInitialData,
    clear,
  };
}
```

import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import { getMarketplace, clearMarketplace } from '../services';
import { MarketplaceStatus } from '../models';

export function useMarketplace() {
  const marketplace = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.marketplace));

  const requestGetMarketplace = useCallback(
    async (params: Record<string, string>) => {
      await getMarketplace(params);
    },
    []
  );

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearMarketplace();
  }, []);

  return {
    data: marketplace.data,
    isLoading: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.LOADING,
    isError: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.ERROR,
    requestGetMarketplace,
    clear,
  };
}
```

12.1 CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND (PRE-EXISTING - NOT CAUSED BY MIGRATION)
================================================================================

These issues existed in the original card-place codebase. They are NOT migration errors but should be documented for future reference. Per migration rules, these should NOT be fixed during migration (no logic changes allowed).

### 12.1.1 rescue.service.ts - Response Validation Logic (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/services/rescue.service.ts:23
Issue: `if (response?.data?.status === 'OK' || response?.status?.message)` uses OR instead of AND
Impact: Any response with a message object could be treated as success
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.2 InvoiceCashbackSummary - campaingId Typo (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx:97
Issue: Route param named `campaingId` (misspelled) but payload field is `campaignId` (correct)
Impact: Field name mismatch could cause data loss if caller uses correct spelling
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.3 InvoiceCashbackOnboarding - pointsQuantity Always 0 (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx:51, parameter.store.ts:26
Issue: pointsQuantity initialized to 0 and never updated (setPointsQuantity never called)
Impact: Display shows "Cada 0 pontos valem R$ 0.00" always
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.4 InvoiceCashbackPoints - Dual conversionFactor Sources (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx:54
Issue: Uses route.params.conversionFactor (defaults to 0) instead of usePoints() hook value
Impact: If route param missing, all conversions silently fail (multiply by 0)
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.5 ExtractRewardProgram - Pagination Race Condition (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/index.ts:285-287
Issue: handleScroll uses refs instead of reactive state, allowing multiple concurrent requests
Impact: Duplicate API requests, data merging bugs, page counter desync
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.6 ExtractRewardProgram - Data Grouping Lost on Append (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:97
Issue: appendExtractData overwrites groupedTransactions with only new page's groups
Impact: Date grouping destroyed on pagination, UI shows incomplete grouped data
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.7 ExtractRewardProgram - "A expirar" Tab Empty (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/view.tsx:176-180
Issue: Second tab renders empty fragment <></>
Impact: Tab is non-functional
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.8 parameter.store.ts - Missing Error Field (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/parameter.store.ts:22-28, 8-15
Issue: No error field in state, no setError method in actions
Impact: Parameter fetch errors cannot be stored or displayed
Status: PRE-EXISTING - design inconsistency from original architecture

### 12.1.9 extract.store.ts - setBalanceStatus Doesn't Clear Error (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:120-123
Issue: setBalanceStatus doesn't clear error on LOADING (unlike setExtractStatus)
Impact: Stale error messages remain visible on retry
Status: PRE-EXISTING - inconsistency within same store

### 12.1.10 products.service.ts - shouldFetchProducts Missing SUCCESS Guard (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/services/products.service.ts:7-10
Issue: Only checks LOADING status, not SUCCESS - allows refetch of cached data
Impact: Unnecessary refetching, no cache respect
Status: PRE-EXISTING - design decision, not a bug

Single useEffect with [isFocused] dependency — a primitive boolean from React Navigation's focus/blur events. Effect only fires on actual focus transitions (true<->false). dispatchEventFocus is an inline closure capturing route, analytic, title, handleAfterDispatch — intentionally excluded from deps via eslint-disable (adding it would fire analytics on every render). Stale closure is benign: all 5 callers pass static string literals, and values are fresh at focus transitions since React re-renders before running effects. handleAfterDispatch is not passed by any current caller; even if it called setState in parent, isFocused would remain unchanged so the effect would not re-fire. The if(isFocused) guard provides a second layer preventing execution on blur. 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict. No corrections needed.)

Store transitions via useShallow cause bounded re-renders (max 3: IDLE→LOADING→SUCCESS/ERROR) that never re-trigger the effect. Non-issues noted: dead refresh state (vestigial), invoiceList() called twice per render (minor perf), non-memoized handlers (child not React.memo), no useEffect cleanup for async (safe with Zustand external state). 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict.)

# Reward Program - Redux to Zustand Migration

Last Updated: 2026-01-28 14:55
Status: COMPLETE - All phases completed (1-13). Redux to Zustand migration finished. All tests pass (113/113). All lint errors resolved (0 errors, 6 warnings). TypeScript check passes.

================================================================================
CRITICAL: PREVIOUS SESSION ERRORS TO AVOID
================================================================================

1. INFRASTRUCTURE WAS CREATED BUT SCREENS WERE NOT MIGRATED
   - Zustand stores exist but screens still use Redux connect()
   - useRescue hook was created but NEVER integrated into screens
   - This made the migration USELESS - data flows through Redux, not Zustand

2. TYPES WERE DUPLICATED UNNECESSARILY
   - PointsProduct already exists in services/models/products.model.ts
   - Do NOT add duplicate types - check if they exist first

3. BALANCE LOCATION WAS CONFUSED
   - Balance is in `pointsBalance` within extract.store.ts
   - NOT in parameter.store.ts
   - The getPointsBalance() service exists in extract.service.ts

4. PRODUCTION PATTERN WAS NOT FOLLOWED
   - Production (card-place) calls services DIRECTLY, not always through hooks
   - Example: getParameterPointsProgram() is called directly in Points screen

5. usePermission USES SuperApp's Redux (customerReducer.nrc)
   - This is NOT local Redux to migrate
   - Production also uses useSelector for this
   - Keep as-is until SuperApp migrates customer data to Zustand

### 2.2 Export from hooks/index.ts ✅ DONE

This hook REPLACES all pointsReducer + PointsActions usage.

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import {
  getPointsProducts,
  clearPointsProducts,
  getPointsBalance,
  getParameterPointsProgram,
} from '../services';
import {
  PointsProductsStatus,
  PointsBalanceStatus,
  ParameterPointsProgramStatus,
} from '../models';

export function usePoints() {
  // Products from products store
  const pointsProducts = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsProducts));
  const { pointsProductsActions } = useRootStore.getState();

  // Balance from extract store (pointsBalance)
  const pointsBalance = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsBalance));

  // Parameterization from parameter store
  const parameterPointsProgram = useRootStore(
    useShallow((state) => state.parameterPointsProgram)
  );

  const requestInitialData = useCallback(async () => {
    await Promise.all([
      getPointsBalance(),
      getPointsProducts(),
      getParameterPointsProgram(),
    ]);
  }, []);

  const requestPointsBalance = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsBalance();
  }, []);

  const requestPointsProducts = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  const requestParameterization = useCallback(async () => {
    await getParameterPointsProgram();
  }, []);

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  return {
    // Balance (from extract store - pointsBalance)
    points: pointsBalance.points,
    isLoadingBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.ERROR,

    // Products (from products store)
    products: pointsProducts.products,
    isLoadingProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.ERROR,

    // Parameterization (from parameter store)
    pointsQuantity: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,
    conversionFactor: parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor,
    isLoadingParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,

    // Actions
    requestPointsBalance,
    requestPointsProducts,
    requestParameterization,
    requestInitialData,
    clear,
  };
}
```

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import { getMarketplace, clearMarketplace } from '../services';
import { MarketplaceStatus } from '../models';

export function useMarketplace() {
  const marketplace = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.marketplace));

  const requestGetMarketplace = useCallback(
    async (params: Record<string, string>) => {
      await getMarketplace(params);
    },
    []
  );

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearMarketplace();
  }, []);

  return {
    data: marketplace.data,
    isLoading: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.LOADING,
    isError: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.ERROR,
    requestGetMarketplace,
    clear,
  };
}
```

Issue: Uses route.params.conversionFactor (defaults to 0) instead of usePoints() hook value
Impact: If route param missing, all conversions silently fail (multiply by 0)
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.5 ExtractRewardProgram - Pagination Race Condition (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/index.ts:285-287
Issue: handleScroll uses refs instead of reactive state, allowing multiple concurrent requests
Impact: Duplicate API requests, data merging bugs, page counter desync
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.6 ExtractRewardProgram - Data Grouping Lost on Append (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:97
Issue: appendExtractData overwrites groupedTransactions with only new page's groups
Impact: Date grouping destroyed on pagination, UI shows incomplete grouped data
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.7 ExtractRewardProgram - "A expirar" Tab Empty (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/view.tsx:176-180
Issue: Second tab renders empty fragment <></>
Impact: Tab is non-functional
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.8 parameter.store.ts - Missing Error Field (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/parameter.store.ts:22-28, 8-15
Issue: No error field in state, no setError method in actions
Impact: Parameter fetch errors cannot be stored or displayed
Status: PRE-EXISTING - design inconsistency from original architecture

### 12.1.9 extract.store.ts - setBalanceStatus Doesn't Clear Error (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:120-123
Issue: setBalanceStatus doesn't clear error on LOADING (unlike setExtractStatus)
Impact: Stale error messages remain visible on retry
Status: PRE-EXISTING - inconsistency within same store

### 12.1.10 products.service.ts - shouldFetchProducts Missing SUCCESS Guard (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/services/products.service.ts:7-10
Issue: Only checks LOADING status, not SUCCESS - allows refetch of cached data
Impact: Unnecessary refetching, no cache respect
Status: PRE-EXISTING - design decision, not a bug

12.2 MIGRATION VERIFICATION RESULTS
================================================================================

### Redux Removal: ✅ VERIFIED COMPLETE
- No `react-redux` imports in src/screens/
- No `connect()` usage in src/screens/
- No `mapStateToProps`/`mapDispatchToProps` in src/
- No `bindActionCreators` in src/
- No `redux/ducks` or `redux/reducers` imports in src/
- src/redux/ folder: DELETED
- Only legitimate Redux reference: usePermission.ts uses useSelector for SuperApp's customerReducer.nrc (expected - SuperApp dependency)

### Zustand Infrastructure: ✅ VERIFIED CORRECT
- All 7 slices follow StateCreator pattern correctly
- setRewardsProgramSlices correctly composes all slices
- clearRewardsProgramStore resets all 7 domains
- Exports properly configured in src/index.ts

### Hook Component Pattern: ✅ VERIFIED ALL SCREENS
- All 10 screens follow index.tsx (logic) + view.tsx (render) pattern
- Types defined in Models/ folders
- No logic in view files

### State Mapping Redux→Zustand: ✅ VERIFIED CORRECT
- pointsReducer.points → pointsBalance.points (extract store)
- pointsReducer.products → pointsProducts.products (products store)
- pointsReducer.pointsQuantity → parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity
- pointsReducer.conversionFactor → parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor
- rescueReducer → rescue store
- getMarketplaceReducer → marketplace store

### Hooks Integration: ✅ VERIFIED CORRECT
- usePoints: Correctly aggregates from products, extract, parameter stores
- useRescue: Correctly wraps rescue store and service
- usePermission: Correctly uses SuperApp Redux + local Zustand
- useMarketplace: Correctly wraps marketplace store and service
- useAirlineMiles: Correctly wraps airlineMiles store and service

================================================================================
12.3 OBSERVATIONS (NOT ISSUES - INTENTIONAL PATTERNS)
================================================================================

1. usePermission uses Redux useSelector for accountId - CORRECT, SuperApp dependency
2. Some screens call services directly instead of through hooks - CORRECT, matches original pattern
3. clear() in usePoints only resets products - CORRECT, matches original cleanup behavior
4. Parameter SUCCESS is set inside setData - CORRECT, consistent with original design

- [x] Deeply and recursively review the possible react hooks inside "src/atomic/atoms/AnimationArrows/index.tsx" against possible infinite loops. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. All 6 Animated.Value refs via useRef().current are referentially stable. useCallback(ArrowAnimate) is stable since all deps are stable refs. useEffect fires only on isFocused change — no state setters inside, no re-render triggers. Animated.Value.setValue()/stopAnimation()/loop().start() operate imperatively outside React reconciliation. 3 review agents: 2 approved, 1 flagged missing useEffect cleanup as resource leak on unmount — PRE-EXISTING code quality issue, not an infinite loop, documented below as future task.)

- [x] Deeply and recursively review the possible react hooks inside "src/atomic/templates/BasicScreen/index.tsx" against possible infinite loops. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. 5 hooks analyzed: useNavigation (stable context ref), useNavigationState (returns primitive string — stable by value), useCallback(nativeGoBackHandler, [hasNativeGoBack, nativeGoBackFn]) (both deps are primitives or external; if parent doesn't memoize nativeGoBackFn, causes BackHandler effect churn but NOT a loop since addEventListener/remove are native bridge calls with no React state mutation), useEffect([props.hasError]) fires eventModalView which is analytics-only with no state side effects (eslint-disable on currentRouteName is intentional — including it would re-fire analytics on navigation while error persists), useEffect([nativeGoBackHandler]) manages BackHandler listener imperatively with cleanup — no state feedback path. handleGoToHome is not memoized but only passed to pure FC BasicScreenView (no loop since child re-render doesn't feed back). 3 review agents: all APPROVED. Observations (non-blocking): unstable nativeGoBackFn from parent causes unnecessary native re-subscriptions, handleGoToHome could be useCallback-wrapped if View were ever React.memo'd.)

- [x] Deeply and recursively review the possible react hooks inside "src/hooks/tracking/index.tsx" against possible infinite loops. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. Single useEffect with [isFocused] dependency — a primitive boolean from React Navigation's focus/blur events. Effect only fires on actual focus transitions (true<->false). dispatchEventFocus is an inline closure capturing route, analytic, title, handleAfterDispatch — intentionally excluded from deps via eslint-disable (adding it would fire analytics on every render). Stale closure is benign: all 5 callers pass static string literals, and values are fresh at focus transitions since React re-renders before running effects. handleAfterDispatch is not passed by any current caller; even if it called setState in parent, isFocused would remain unchanged so the effect would not re-fire. The if(isFocused) guard provides a second layer preventing execution on blur. 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict. No corrections needed.)

- [x] Deeply and recursively review the possible react hooks inside "src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx" against possible infinite loops. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. 7 hooks analyzed: usePoints() (custom hook with 3 useShallow selectors + 5 useCallback([]) singletons — all referentially stable), useState(0) refresh with no setter (constant 0, dead code), useRef(false) hasFocused (ref guard for analytics), useFocusEffect(useCallback(fn, [])) (stable callback, fires once per focus with ref guard, no state mutations), useNavigation() (stable context ref), useCallback(requestAll, [requestParameterization, requestPointsProducts]) (both deps are useCallback([]) singletons — frozen), useEffect([requestParameterization, refresh]) (fires exactly once on mount — requestParameterization is useCallback([]) singleton and refresh is constant 0; three-layer defense: stable deps + immutable refresh + service LOADING/SUCCESS guard). Store transitions via useShallow cause bounded re-renders (max 3: IDLE→LOADING→SUCCESS/ERROR) that never re-trigger the effect. Non-issues noted: dead refresh state (vestigial), invoiceList() called twice per render (minor perf), non-memoized handlers (child not React.memo), no useEffect cleanup for async (safe with Zustand external state). 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict.)

- [x] Deeply and recursively review the possible react hooks inside "src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx" against possible infinite loops. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. 14 hooks analyzed (including nested hooks from usePoints and useScreenViewTracking). Key findings: (1) useEffect([requestParameterization]) fires exactly once on mount — requestParameterization is useCallback([]) singleton, referentially stable; getParameterPointsProgram service has LOADING/SUCCESS guard preventing re-entry; store transitions (IDLE→LOADING→SUCCESS) cause bounded 3 re-renders but never re-trigger the effect. (2) Second useFocusEffect with [setPointsValue, setReaisValue] deps is safe — React guarantees useState setters are referentially stable, making this equivalent to useCallback([]); state resets fire on focus/blur only (not on re-renders) and no effect depends on pointsValue/reaisValue/pointsError/reaisError. (3) First useFocusEffect with [] deps + hasFocused ref guard — fires analytics once per focus, no state mutations. (4) useScreenViewTracking internal useEffect([isFocused]) — fires on boolean primitive toggle, analytics only, no state mutations, eslint-disable on dispatchEventFocus is intentional. (5) useMemo([conversionFactor]) — depends on route params (frozen per navigation entry). Non-blocking observations: invoiceList() creates new array every render (could be useMemo'd), Intl.NumberFormat instantiated every render, 8 unmemoized handler functions, Dimensions.get('window') called every render instead of useWindowDimensions(), duplicate analytics on focus (useScreenViewTracking V2 + useFocusEffect V3 — may be intentional dual-system). 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict with HIGH confidence.)
